title: bromus sterilis var. fasciculatus
intro: Bromus sterilis var. fasciculatus, also known as barren brome or fascicled brome, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass that grows in the wild, particularly in dry and sandy areas, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its astringent and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Bromus sterilis var. fasciculatus can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
